programme.sub-title: Saka or Salah? Foden or Fernandes? Wilson or Watkins? Picking your Fantasy team is never easy but this season the Fantasy Show is on hand to help you with those big decisions. programme.title: PREVIEW FRI 07 MAR
programme.sub-title: This week’s show looks ahead to Matchweek 28 and the weekend opener of Nottingham Forest versus Manchester City. Followed by the in-form Brentford taking on Aston Villa. Then Monday’s kick off closes out the Matchweek, with West Ham facing Newcastle at London Stadium.
programme.desc: This week’s show looks ahead to Matchweek 28 and the weekend opener of Nottingham Forest versus Manchester City. Followed by the in-form Brentford taking on Aston Villa. Then Monday’s kick off closes out the Matchweek, with West Ham facing Newcastle at London Stadium.

programme.title: WELCOME TO THE WEEKEND FRI 07 MAR
programme.sub-title: Arsenal centre-backs Gabriel and Saliba meet Emil for a hilarious game of Buzz Off, Olivia chats to Manchester United's Andre Onana alongside Wigan Warriors rugby player Junior Nsemba, plus we're on the road as Nottingham Forest get set to host Manchester City.
programme.desc: Arsenal centre-backs Gabriel and Saliba meet Emil for a hilarious game of Buzz Off, Olivia chats to Manchester United's Andre Onana alongside Wigan Warriors rugby player Junior Nsemba, plus we're on the road as Nottingham Forest get set to host Manchester City.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP30: (Subbed) MURILLO
programme.sub-title: Nottingham Forest are enjoying their best season in the Premier League in 30 years, and a large part of that is down to one of the league’s most formidable defenders. Murillo talks about Forest’s incredible season as well as some of the experiences in his life which have made him such a tough opponent. A change of allegiance from the club he supported as a boy to their rivals, developing his game by playing Futsal and the loss of his father when he was just 10 years-old are just some of the stories that Murillo shares in this week’s Big Interview.
programme.desc: Nottingham Forest are enjoying their best season in the Premier League in 30 years, and a large part of that is down to one of the league’s most formidable defenders. Murillo talks about Forest’s incredible season as well as some of the experiences in his life which have made him such a tough opponent. A change of allegiance from the club he supported as a boy to their rivals, developing his game by playing Futsal and the loss of his father when he was just 10 years-old are just some of the stories that Murillo shares in this week’s Big Interview.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP40: INTERNATIONAL WOMAN’S DAY
programme.sub-title: The Premier League’s success is down to all the incredible people working tirelessly on all different aspects of one of the best leagues in the world. An increasing number of these people are women, and we want to highlight the achievements of those who work behind the scenes at Premier League clubs. We follow Brentford analyst Haydee Agras, Ipswich’s Soft Tissue Specialist Alice Grindrod, Liverpool’s Clare Farrell and Bournemouth Media Relations Officer Zoe Rundle at their training grounds while Women in Football CEO Yvonne Harrison gives us the wider context of the success of women in the game.
programme.desc: The Premier League’s success is down to all the incredible people working tirelessly on all different aspects of one of the best leagues in the world. An increasing number of these people are women, and we want to highlight the achievements of those who work behind the scenes at Premier League clubs. We follow Brentford analyst Haydee Agras, Ipswich’s Soft Tissue Specialist Alice Grindrod, Liverpool’s Clare Farrell and Bournemouth Media Relations Officer Zoe Rundle at their training grounds while Women in Football CEO Yvonne Harrison gives us the wider context of the success of women in the game.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP07: FERGIE v WENGER
programme.sub-title: Widely regarded as the two best managers in Premier League history, Sir Alex Ferguson and Arsene Wenger went toe to toe on the touchline on numerous occasions over the years. But what made their rivalry so fierce? We take a look at the intense clashes, memorable moments on and off the pitch and the monumental impact their duels had on English football's modern era.

programme.title: MATCHDAY LIVE Nottingham Forest v Manchester City / Liverpool v Southampton (Goal Rush) / Brentford v Aston Villa / Wolverhampton Wanderers v Everton
programme.sub-title: Matchday Live goes On The Road to The City Ground as Nottingham Forest host Champions Manchester City. Michael Owen, Shaun Wight-Phillips and Martin O’Neill will be pitch-side to bring you analysis and reaction. Then, Tim Sherwood and Rachel Corsie are in studio for Liverpool against Southampton headlining Goal Rush, Brentford versus Aston Villa and the late game between Wolves and Everton on a bumper day of Premier League action.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP35: ANDY CARROLL
programme.sub-title: PL Stories follows the journey of Andy Carroll, from his high-flying Premier League career to his new chapter in Bordeaux. The documentary captures Carroll’s resilience as he navigates the challenges of his football career, reflecting on the ups and downs along the way. We join him in Bordeaux, where he looks back on his past, his passion for the game, and his drive to prove himself once again

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP30: (Subbed) MURILLO
programme.sub-title: Nottingham Forest are enjoying their best season in the Premier League in 30 years, and a large part of that is down to one of the league’s most formidable defenders. Murillo talks about Forest’s incredible season as well as some of the experiences in his life which have made him such a tough opponent. A change of allegiance from the club he supported as a boy to their rivals, developing his game by playing Futsal and the loss of his father when he was just 10 years-old are just some of the stories that Murillo shares in this week’s Big Interview.
programme.desc: Nottingham Forest are enjoying their best season in the Premier League in 30 years, and a large part of that is down to one of the league’s most formidable defenders. Murillo talks about Forest’s incredible season as well as some of the experiences in his life which have made him such a tough opponent. A change of allegiance from the club he supported as a boy to their rivals, developing his game by playing Futsal and the loss of his father when he was just 10 years-old are just some of the stories that Murillo shares in this week’s Big Interview.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP40: INTERNATIONAL WOMAN’S DAY
programme.sub-title: The Premier League’s success is down to all the incredible people working tirelessly on all different aspects of one of the best leagues in the world. An increasing number of these people are women, and we want to highlight the achievements of those who work behind the scenes at Premier League clubs. We follow Brentford analyst Haydee Agras, Ipswich’s Soft Tissue Specialist Alice Grindrod, Liverpool’s Clare Farrell and Bournemouth Media Relations Officer Zoe Rundle at their training grounds while Women in Football CEO Yvonne Harrison gives us the wider context of the success of women in the game.
programme.desc: The Premier League’s success is down to all the incredible people working tirelessly on all different aspects of one of the best leagues in the world. An increasing number of these people are women, and we want to highlight the achievements of those who work behind the scenes at Premier League clubs. We follow Brentford analyst Haydee Agras, Ipswich’s Soft Tissue Specialist Alice Grindrod, Liverpool’s Clare Farrell and Bournemouth Media Relations Officer Zoe Rundle at their training grounds while Women in Football CEO Yvonne Harrison gives us the wider context of the success of women in the game.

programme.title: PL REWIND EP14: SLAVEN BILIC: MY WEST HAM STORY
programme.sub-title: A West Ham cult hero, Slaven Bilic first represented the Irons as a player in the 1990s. He returned to the club as manager in 2015, ahead of the Hammers’ final season at Upton Park. The Croatian looks back on what proved to be a remarkable campaign in East London.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP22: HOWARD WEBB
programme.sub-title: Head of the PGMOL, Howard Webb is in the studio for this week’s Big Interview, candidly opening up about his personal story and how he got into the game. Webb reflects on various moments throughout his life and career including that infamous decision not to issue a red card in the 2010 World Cup Final, implementing VAR and where he sees the technology going, as well as his hopes that referees will continue to be more inclusive in the Premier League.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP34: (Subbed) CESC FABREGAS
programme.sub-title: A product of Barcelona’s famed La Masia youth academy, Cesc Fabregas is rightly remembered as one of the greatest midfield players of his generation. After making his Arsenal debut at just 16 years of age the diminutive Spaniard would go on to win titles in both England and Spain as well as becoming a World Cup winner in 2010. Now taking his first steps into coaching with Como 1907, Fabregas reflects on his footballing education as well as his hopes for the future in management.
programme.desc: A product of Barcelona’s famed La Masia youth academy, Cesc Fabregas is rightly remembered as one of the greatest midfield players of his generation. After making his Arsenal debut at just 16 years of age the diminutive Spaniard would go on to win titles in both England and Spain as well as becoming a World Cup winner in 2010. Now taking his first steps into coaching with Como 1907, Fabregas reflects on his footballing education as well as his hopes for the future in management.

programme.sub-title: The Premier League’s success is down to all the incredible people working tirelessly on all different aspects of one of the best leagues in the world. An increasing number of these people are women, and we want to highlight the achievements of those who work behind the scenes at Premier League clubs. We follow Brentford analyst Haydee Agras, Ipswich’s Soft Tissue Specialist Alice Grindrod, Liverpool’s Clare Farrell and Bournemouth Media Relations Officer Zoe Rundle at their training grounds while Women in Football CEO Yvonne Harrison gives us the wider context of the success of women in the game.
programme.desc: The Premier League’s success is down to all the incredible people working tirelessly on all different aspects of one of the best leagues in the world. An increasing number of these people are women, and we want to highlight the achievements of those who work behind the scenes at Premier League clubs. We follow Brentford analyst Haydee Agras, Ipswich’s Soft Tissue Specialist Alice Grindrod, Liverpool’s Clare Farrell and Bournemouth Media Relations Officer Zoe Rundle at their training grounds while Women in Football CEO Yvonne Harrison gives us the wider context of the success of women in the game.

programme.sub-title: In this episode of 1-to-11, the legend that is Claude Makelele selects his ultimate Premier League One to Eleven, featuring the very best players he played with and against. programme.title: PL STORIES EP34: (Subbed) CESC FABREGAS
programme.sub-title: A product of Barcelona’s famed La Masia youth academy, Cesc Fabregas is rightly remembered as one of the greatest midfield players of his generation. After making his Arsenal debut at just 16 years of age the diminutive Spaniard would go on to win titles in both England and Spain as well as becoming a World Cup winner in 2010. Now taking his first steps into coaching with Como 1907, Fabregas reflects on his footballing education as well as his hopes for the future in management.
programme.desc: A product of Barcelona’s famed La Masia youth academy, Cesc Fabregas is rightly remembered as one of the greatest midfield players of his generation. After making his Arsenal debut at just 16 years of age the diminutive Spaniard would go on to win titles in both England and Spain as well as becoming a World Cup winner in 2010. Now taking his first steps into coaching with Como 1907, Fabregas reflects on his footballing education as well as his hopes for the future in management.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP37: (Subbed) NANI
programme.sub-title: A player of flair and flamboyance for Sir Alex Ferguson’s Manchester United, the story of Nani from the streets of Lisbon is one of defying the odds. Nani retraces his footsteps across Portugal’s capital, visiting old clubs and former coaches, the neighbourhood he learnt to survive in, as well as new ventures afoot.
programme.desc: A player of flair and flamboyance for Sir Alex Ferguson’s Manchester United, the story of Nani from the streets of Lisbon is one of defying the odds. programme.sub-title: A relationship steeped in glory, Chelsea Football Club is the Italian Job. Last summer, Enzo Maresca became the seventh manager from that heritage to take charge at Stamford Bridge. programme.title: PL STORIES EP39: (Subbed) ARSENE WENGER AND HIS MANAGERIAL JOURNEY
programme.sub-title: We explore Arsène Wenger's managerial journey before joining Arsenal, tracing his evolution from a young, innovative coach in France to a transformative force in European football. Delving into his time at Nancy, AS Monaco and Nagoya Grampus Eight, Wenger's tactical brilliance began to shape his future success. We highlight the key moments that led him to one of football's most iconic roles.
